touches.ai_summary: LinkedIn message sent to Parker Yoon introducing our CRM automation platform and requesting a brief conversation about RevOps challenges at CoastalRelay. No response received.
Cold call attempt to Parker Yoon. Left voicemail mentioning how we've helped similar fintech companies automate their sales workflows. No callback received.
Brief call with Parker who answered between meetings. Confirmed interest in exploring CRM automation solutions. Scheduled qualification call for next week.

questions_asked.question: Are you currently using any CRM automation tools?
questions_asked.answer: We have basic workflows in Salesforce but nothing sophisticated. I just joined as Head of RevOps and see major gaps.

next_steps: Schedule 30-minute qualification call for next Tuesday

touches.ai_summary: Qualification call with Parker covering current RevOps stack and pain points. CoastalRelay manually routing leads and lacks automation for follow-up sequences. Parker is 2 months into role and building business case for improvements.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the urgency to solve this now?
questions_asked.answer: Our sales team is growing fast—we've added 15 reps in 6 months. Manual processes don't scale and we're losing deals in the cracks.

questions_asked.question: Can your platform integrate with our existing Salesforce instance?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have native Salesforce integration. We can map your existing fields and workflows.

next_steps: Parker to map current workflow gaps
Schedule discovery deep-dive

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up qualification call to review Parker's workflow mapping. Identified 3 key automation opportunities: lead routing, email sequences, and opportunity scoring. Discussed budget range and decision process.

questions_asked.question: Who else needs to be involved in evaluating a solution like this?
questions_asked.answer: Nicole Murphy in Sales and Rosa Koch in IT will need to sign off. I own the budget but they'll want to see it in action.

questions_asked.question: What's the typical implementation timeline?
questions_asked.answer: Most mid-market customers are live in 4-6 weeks depending on complexity.

next_steps: Begin formal discovery with detailed use case review

touches.ai_summary: Called Parker to confirm discovery meeting time. Went to voicemail, left message confirming Thursday slot.
Email to Parker with discovery call prep materials and questionnaire about current CRM workflows. Parker responded confirming receipt and will complete before call.
next_steps: Parker to complete discovery questionnaire
Discovery call Thursday 2pm

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sharing case study of similar fintech company that automated lead routing and increased conversion by 34%. Parker replied expressing interest in similar outcomes.

questions_asked.question: Is that fintech company also in the SMB lending space?
questions_asked.answer: They focus on payment processing, but the CRM automation challenges are very similar—high volume, fast follow-up requirements.

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call reviewing Parker's completed questionnaire. Deep dive into lead routing logic, current response times (averaging 4+ hours), and manual data entry burden on sales team.

questions_asked.question: What percentage of inbound leads currently get any automated follow-up?
questions_asked.answer: Honestly, almost none. Reps are manually sending first emails. It's completely unsustainable.

questions_asked.question: Can we customize the automation rules by region or product line?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely. You can build conditional logic based on any field in your CRM.

next_steps: Rep to draft solution architecture
Schedule technical discovery with Rosa from IT

touches.ai_summary: Second discovery call focused on data hygiene and reporting needs. Discussed how automated workflows can enforce data standards and provide visibility into pipeline health.

questions_asked.question: How does your platform handle duplicate detection and merging?
questions_asked.answer: We have built-in deduplication logic that runs on record creation and can auto-merge or flag for review based on your rules.

questions_asked.question: What reports does your sales leadership look at weekly?
questions_asked.answer: Pipeline coverage, response times, and lead source performance. Right now we're building those manually in spreadsheets.

next_steps: Include reporting templates in demo preparation

touches.ai_summary: Extended discovery session mapping end-to-end automation for CoastalRelay's inbound and outbound motions. Parker shared that exec leadership is pushing for better pipeline predictability. Identified 10-seat requirement.

questions_asked.question: Beyond the immediate RevOps and Sales teams, who else might benefit from these automations?
questions_asked.answer: Potentially customer success down the road, but for now let's focus on the 10 sales and RevOps users.

questions_asked.question: What level of support comes with the platform?
questions_asked.answer: You get dedicated CSM, implementation support, and 24/7 technical support with our standard plans.

questions_asked.question: Can we start with a smaller scope and expand later?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can phase the rollout, but the unlimited automation tier gives you flexibility to scale without re-negotiating.

next_steps: Prepare customized demo for CoastalRelay use cases
Invite Nicole and Rosa to demo

touches.ai_summary: Product demo with Parker walking through lead routing automation, email sequences, and real-time reporting dashboards. Parker engaged throughout, asking detailed questions about customization. Nicole joined for last 15 minutes.

questions_asked.question: Can we A/B test different email sequences?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, the platform supports multivariate testing on email content, timing, and sender.

questions_asked.question: How quickly can we modify automation rules once we're live?
questions_asked.answer: Changes take effect immediately. You have a visual workflow builder—no dev resources needed.

questions_asked.question: Nicole, what are your top priorities for sales automation?
questions_asked.answer: Making sure reps spend time selling, not doing data entry. This looks like it could save us 5-10 hours per rep per week.

next_steps: Schedule technical evaluation with Rosa
Parker to share demo recording with broader team

touches.ai_summary: Technical evaluation call with Parker and Rosa Koch (IT Director). Covered security, data privacy, API limits, and integration architecture. Rosa asked detailed questions about SSO, audit logging, and user permissions.

questions_asked.question: Do you support SAML-based SSO?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we integrate with all major identity providers including Okta, Azure AD, and Google Workspace.

questions_asked.question: What data residency options do you offer?
questions_asked.answer: We have US and EU data centers. For fintech customers we typically recommend US-based hosting with SOC 2 compliance.

questions_asked.question: Rosa, any technical blockers you're seeing?
questions_asked.answer: No major concerns. I'll need to review your security documentation but initial review looks solid.

objections: Need to complete internal security review before moving forward
next_steps: Send SOC 2 report and security whitepaper to Rosa
Rosa to complete security assessment within 1 week

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up demo focusing on advanced automation scenarios Parker requested. Showed workflow templates for fintech companies and discussed implementation approach. Parker confirmed security review in progress.

questions_asked.question: What does the implementation process look like from contract to go-live?
questions_asked.answer: Kickoff within 1 week, data mapping and configuration over 2-3 weeks, testing and training in week 4, then go-live with our support team on standby.

questions_asked.question: When are you hoping to have this live?
questions_asked.answer: Ideally before end of Q2. We have a major sales kickoff in June and want this in place beforehand.

next_steps: Prepare formal proposal with pricing
Target proposal review next week pending security clearance

touches.ai_summary: Scheduled proposal review call with Parker. Parker requested to reschedule 20 minutes before the meeting due to conflicting exec meeting.
next_steps: Reschedule proposal call

touches.ai_summary: Proposal review call with Parker. Presented $9,400 ACV for 10 seats with unlimited automation tier. Parker seemed positive but noted Nicole and Rosa need to review before he can commit. No exec sponsor involved yet.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on the annual commitment? Can we do quarterly?
questions_asked.answer: Our standard terms are annual, but the pricing reflects that commitment. Quarterly would be 20% higher per quarter.

questions_asked.question: What's your internal approval process from here?
questions_asked.answer: I need buy-in from Nicole and Rosa, then I can approve up to $15K. Should be straightforward.

objections: Would prefer quarterly payment terms if possible
next_steps: Parker to circulate proposal with Nicole and Rosa
Regroup next week with feedback

touches.ai_summary: Email to Parker following up on proposal review. Parker responded that Rosa cleared security review and Nicole is reviewing ROI internally. Expects feedback by end of week.
next_steps: Wait for feedback from Nicole and Rosa
Parker to schedule alignment call when ready

touches.ai_summary: Extended meeting with Parker to address questions from Nicole and Rosa. Nicole asked about ROI measurement and reporting. Rosa confirmed technical approval. Parker mentioned he hasn't looped in his VP yet but feels confident in budget approval. Discussed contract timeline.

questions_asked.question: Can we include custom ROI dashboards in the implementation?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we build those as part of onboarding. We'll track the metrics that matter to your leadership team.

questions_asked.question: What happens if we need to add seats mid-year?
questions_asked.answer: We pro-rate additional seats based on remaining contract term. Most customers add 20-30% more seats in year one.

questions_asked.question: Should we get your VP involved before finalizing?
questions_asked.answer: I don't think it's necessary—this is well within my authority. But I can loop her in if it becomes an issue.

next_steps: Parker to confirm final budget approval internally
Target contract send by end of week

touches.ai_summary: Scheduled exec alignment meeting to finalize terms. Parker rescheduled day-of due to competing priority. Expressed apologies and proposed time next week.
next_steps: Reschedule exec meeting
touches.reschedule_reason: prospect asked to push by a week

touches.ai_summary: Email checking in with Parker on rescheduling. Parker responded apologizing for delay and confirmed budget is approved but procurement (Nicole) is pushing back on annual commitment and wants quarterly terms.
next_steps: Discuss payment term options
Schedule call to address procurement concerns

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email to Parker sharing flexible payment option: annual contract with quarterly invoicing at 10% premium. Parker acknowledged and said he'd discuss with Nicole.
next_steps: Parker to review payment option with Nicole

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call with Parker and Nicole Murphy. Nicole expressed concern about committing $9,400 without exec-level validation given Parker's tenure. Discussed payment terms and Nicole requested 15% discount. Parker advocated for the solution but acknowledged he should get VP sign-off. Agreed to annual contract with quarterly invoicing, pending exec approval next week.

questions_asked.question: Can you offer a discount if we commit to annual with quarterly invoicing?
questions_asked.answer: We can hold pricing at the annual rate—no 20% premium—if you commit to the full year. That's effectively a discount on quarterly terms.

questions_asked.question: Nicole, what else do you need to feel comfortable moving forward?
questions_asked.answer: Honestly, I'd feel better if Parker's VP weighed in. No offense Parker, but this is a big commitment and you're still ramping.

questions_asked.question: What's your standard payment term—net 30?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, net 30 on all invoices.

objections: Prefer quarterly invoicing over single annual payment
next_steps: Parker to schedule meeting with VP for exec approval
Finalize contract pending VP alignment
